name: Data Pipeline Test Suite — File I/O and HTTP Integration Tests
scenario: Write a comprehensive test suite for a data pipeline that reads product CSVs, calls a pricing API to enrich each product, and writes a formatted JSON report. The pipeline combines everything from Module 11 — file reading, JSON processing, and HTTP calls. Tests use @TempDir for file operations and Mockito for HTTP mocking.
description: The `ProductEnrichmentPipeline` reads a CSV, calls a price enrichment API for each product, and writes a JSON report. Students write tests covering happy paths, file error paths, HTTP error paths, partial failures, and edge cases — using @TempDir and mocked HttpClient throughout.

core.description: Happy path test: write a 3-product CSV to @TempDir, mock API to return prices, run pipeline, assert JSON report exists and contains all 3 enriched products with correct prices.
Empty CSV test: header-only CSV → empty JSON array in output, pipeline returns 0 enriched count.
Missing input file test: pipeline throws NoSuchFileException or IOException for non-existent CSV path.
API 404 for one product: product with API 404 gets price=0 (default), others are enriched correctly. Pipeline continues despite partial failure.
API IOException for one product: network failure on one SKU → same graceful handling as 404. Other products still enriched.
Report format verification: read the output JSON from @TempDir, parse with Jackson, assert each product has: sku, name, qty, apiPrice fields.
URL verification test: use ArgumentCaptor to capture the HttpRequest and verify the URL contains the product SKU.
All tests use @TempDir for file isolation and @Mock HttpClient. At least 6 test methods total.

bonus.description: Add a @ParameterizedTest using @MethodSource to test multiple CSV files (different column counts, empty rows, headers-only, malformed rows). Each case uses @TempDir.
Add a concurrency test: call pipeline.sync() from 5 threads simultaneously with the same @TempDir. Verify the report is complete and correct (no partial writes, no duplicates).
Add a StringWriter-based test: refactor pipeline to accept a Writer for output. Test using StringWriter — no file needed for the output side of the test.

data_to_use:
Input CSV (products.csv):
sku,name,qty
P001,Wireless Mouse,50
P002,Keyboard,12
P003,Monitor,5

API endpoint: GET /api/prices/{sku}
Success response: {"sku":"P001","price":799}
404 response for unknown SKU

Output JSON: products.json
[{"sku":"P001","name":"Wireless Mouse","qty":50,"apiPrice":799}, ...]
example_output:
Test Results:
✓ enrich_3products_allSucceed_jsonReportCorrect
✓ enrich_emptyCsv_emptyJsonOutput
✓ enrich_missingFile_throwsException
✓ enrich_oneProduct404_continuesOthers
✓ enrich_networkError_gracefulDegradation
✓ enrich_verifyCorrectApiUrl

6 tests passed, 0 failed

criteria: Jackson parse of output JSON (8 pts)
ArgumentCaptor verifies SKU in URL (10 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. File tests: write CSV to tempDir, run pipeline, read JSON from tempDir, assert with Jackson. HTTP partial failure: when(httpResponse.statusCode()).thenReturn(200, 404) — first product 200, second 404. URL captor: verify(client).send(captor.capture(), any()); assertTrue(captor.getValue().uri().toString().contains(sku)).
submission_instructions: Save as ProductEnrichmentPipelineTest.java. Push to GitHub. This combines all Module 11 + Module 12 testing skills.
tips_for_success: Both CSV input and JSON output paths in same @TempDir
For partial failure: when(response.statusCode()).thenReturn(200, 404) — Mockito returns 200 first call, 404 second call
Empty CSV: Files.writeString(path, 'header1,header2\n') — header with trailing newline
